from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from keras.models import Sequential
from keras.layers import Dense
from keras.optimizers import SGD

def task_func(X, Y):
    # Split the data into training and test sets
    X_train, X_test, Y_train, Y_test = train_test_split(X, Y, test_size=0.25, random_state=42)

    # Construct the Sequential model
    model = Sequential()
    model.add(Dense(16, activation='sigmoid', input_shape=(2,)))  # Hidden layer with 16 neurons and sigmoid activation
    model.add(Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))  # Output layer with 1 neuron and sigmoid activation (for binary classification)

    # Compile the model with binary cross-entropy loss and SGD optimizer
    learning_rate = 0.01  # You can adjust this value as needed
    model.compile(loss='binary_crossentropy', optimizer=SGD(lr=learning_rate), metrics=['accuracy'])

    # Fit the model to the training data and evaluate it on the test set (validation)
    history = model.fit(X_train, Y_train, validation_data=(X_test, Y_test), epochs=100, verbose=False)

    # Plot the model's training and validation loss over epochs
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    ax = plt.plot(history.history['loss'], label='Train')
    ax = plt.plot(history.history['val_loss'], label='Test')
    ax.set_title('Model loss')
    ax.set_xlabel('Epoch')
    ax.set_ylabel('Loss')
    ax.legend()

    return model, ax
